title: Any Given Sundance

jokes.text: Behold the tailgate party, the pinnacle of human achievement!
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic declaration about tailgating
jokes.punchline: Calling parking lot drinking the 'pinnacle of human achievement'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer grandiosity about something mundane. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: What child birth is to women, eating trunk meats is to the bewanged.
jokes.setup: Homer making a profound comparison about tailgating
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous word 'bewanged' and the absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Peak Simpsons wordplay with 'bewanged' - original, memorable, and perfectly structured comparison.

jokes.text: What could be greater than eating and drinking for hours in a drizzly parking lot? - Anything. - No. Everything is better. - Anything! - Everything!
jokes.setup: Homer's rhetorical question about tailgating being perfect
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate deflation and their absurd argument over semantics
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing their different perspectives, though the argument gets a bit repetitive.

jokes.text: Daddy, I made a Ralphwich!
jokes.setup: Ralph showing off something he made
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'Ralphwich' combining his name with sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum wordplay - innocent, creative, and character-specific.

jokes.text: It tastes hurty!
jokes.setup: Ralph describing the taste of his creation
jokes.punchline: The childish made-up word 'hurty'
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph childish language, though less inventive than 'Ralphwich'.

jokes.text: No, no, no. I'm sorry. It's food. It's food. Everything's what you say it is.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum initially correcting Ralph about eating non-food
jokes.punchline: His immediate backtrack into enabling parenting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's enabling parenting style and fear of confrontation.

jokes.text: When you know the people you're begging from, it's mooching.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if they're there to beg for food
jokes.punchline: Homer's semantic distinction between begging and mooching
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - making a meaningless distinction sound reasonable. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Moleman! Come here, ya big bag o' skin!
jokes.setup: Homer greeting Moleman at the tailgate
jokes.punchline: The backhanded 'compliment' calling him a 'big bag o' skin'
jokes.explanation: Funny insult disguised as friendly greeting, though somewhat crude.

jokes.text: Springfield U sucks! A&M cheerleaders are clumsy! Your superstar professors treat undergrads like an afterthought! Your tenure track is heavily politicized!
jokes.setup: Lisa joining in sports trash talk
jokes.punchline: Her academic-focused insults escalating to university politics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character comedy - she can't help but make intellectual trash talk. Great escalation structure.

jokes.text: Well, I guess I could cut loose and do a little homework.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Lisa join in the fun
jokes.punchline: Lisa's idea of 'cutting loose' is homework
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa character trait - her version of rebellion is still academic.

jokes.text: I am a born film maker!
jokes.setup: Lisa filming mundane family moments
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose self-declaration about filming ordinary things
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Lisa's confidence, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Three points out of a possible five. Ah, that's a funny joke, Mr. Kincaid. Is this an art class or a comedy class?
jokes.setup: Lisa getting a mediocre grade and thinking teacher is joking
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic response about art vs comedy class
jokes.explanation: Good student-teacher dynamic joke, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I can make it a two. - Three it is!
jokes.setup: Teacher threatening to lower Lisa's grade
jokes.punchline: Lisa's immediate acceptance of the original grade
jokes.explanation: Nice quick reversal joke with good economy - Lisa's pride vs pragmatism.

jokes.text: It's as if Ingmar Bergman and Penny Marshall had a baby.
jokes.setup: Skinner praising Lisa's filmmaking
jokes.punchline: The absurd pairing of arthouse and mainstream directors as parents
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference joke pairing highbrow and lowbrow filmmakers - unexpectedly sophisticated for Skinner.

jokes.text: When Edna Met Seymour, Ghost Willie, Killing Seymour's Mother, The Principal Who Sold a Screenplay.
jokes.setup: Skinner listing his screenplay titles
jokes.punchline: The increasingly personal and meta movie titles
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of parody movie titles getting more specific to Skinner's life.

jokes.text: Orlando or Hollywood? There's one in Hollywood?!
jokes.setup: Skinner mentioning he's not welcome on Universal Studios tour
jokes.punchline: His surprise that there's a Hollywood location, not just Orlando
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's limited knowledge of Hollywood despite his film passion.

jokes.text: Not the smarty-pants answer. Where's the drama in your life? - My family! - Language, rhythm and spectacle.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking for practical answer about drama
jokes.punchline: Lisa continuing with Aristotelian elements despite being told not to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character trait - she literally cannot stop herself from giving the academic answer.

jokes.text: All I ask is, if you ever win an Oscar... give it to me.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking how she can repay Skinner's generosity
jokes.punchline: His modest request for her potential Oscar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's desperate desire for recognition disguised as modesty.

jokes.text: Those cabinetmakers laughed at me, but they'll see.
jokes.setup: Skinner's mysterious muttering
jokes.punchline: The random grudge against cabinetmakers
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Skinner moment - the specificity of 'cabinetmakers' makes it funny.

jokes.text: Can I be Smithers? - No, Dad, I want you to play Homer Simpson. - Why, 'cause I'm fat?
jokes.setup: Lisa telling Homer to play himself in the documentary
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware but wrong assumption about why
jokes.explanation: Decent self-referential humor, though Homer's weight jokes are somewhat tired.

jokes.text: What, I'm going for a Shia LaBeouf thing. Not quite a nerd, not quite a hunk... Shia LaBeouf!
jokes.setup: Lisa telling everyone not to wear toupees
jokes.punchline: Bart's specific analysis of Shia LaBeouf's appeal
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural reference but somewhat dated and not particularly insightful.

jokes.text: Uh-oh, there's already an on-set romance.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge being affectionate during filming
jokes.punchline: Bart treating his parents like actors having an affair
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about film production tropes applied to a married couple.

jokes.text: Homie, she's not making a snuggle film.
jokes.setup: Homer being romantic during documentary filming
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent euphemism for adult films
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - her innocent way of referencing adult content.

jokes.text: I'm thrilled! Or would have been, if not for your knee-jerk assumption that I'm angry at you, which I am now.
jokes.setup: Chalmers appearing to praise Skinner's work
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of becoming angry because Skinner assumed he was angry
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chalmers character moment - perfectly captures his capricious nature and circular logic.

jokes.text: The whole school is made up of modeling clay. Every afternoon, they reshape it just to get my goat.
jokes.setup: Chalmers explaining his rivalry with the arts school
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim about the building being made of clay shaped to annoy him
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific image that perfectly captures Chalmers' paranoid personality.

jokes.text: Banff? Banff, Shmanff. I'm talking about Sundance!
jokes.setup: Skinner suggesting the modest Banff film festival
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' dismissal and ambitious jump to Sundance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chalmers' grandiose thinking, though the 'shmanff' construction is predictable.

jokes.text: If Lisa's movie gets in, we won't just be the school with the highest hamster mortality rate. Ah, so young. We'll also have a kid who did a thing.
jokes.setup: Chalmers explaining what the school will gain
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of dead hamsters and his touching reaction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about dead hamsters mixed with oddly touching moment and vague achievement description.

jokes.text: Paul Giamatti... is the world's greatest superspy... who only exists in the mind of an overweight agoraphobic jazz musician... played by Martin Lawrence in a fat suit.
jokes.setup: Reading a typical indie film premise
jokes.punchline: The increasingly convoluted and contradictory concept
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious indie film concepts, with the Martin Lawrence twist being particularly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, and she's eight years old!
jokes.setup: Building up Lisa's impressive credentials
jokes.punchline: Final shocking detail about her age
jokes.explanation: Good escalation payoff - age revelation explains the judges' excitement

jokes.text: Save the Children? Save your breath. Take a hike, Sierra Club. Doctors without Borders? Doctors without money!
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting charity solicitations
jokes.punchline: His increasingly elaborate rejections, especially the Doctors pun
jokes.explanation: Good escalating series with clever wordplay on 'Doctors without Borders' - classic Homer selfishness.

jokes.text: Robert Redford? Guess what. A scissor runs through it.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to reject mail from charities/celebrities
jokes.punchline: His play on 'A River Runs Through It' with 'scissor'
jokes.explanation: Clever movie title pun showing Homer's destructive intent toward mail - good wordplay.

jokes.text: Aye, los DVDs! With director's commentary and deleted scenes!
jokes.setup: Hearing about film festival
jokes.punchline: His focus on DVD extras rather than the actual films
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but not particularly insightful or memorable.

jokes.text: Who are you? I try to answer that question in my films. What else? I can eat a raw onion without crying.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Jim Jarmusch who he is
jokes.punchline: His pretentious answer followed by random onion fact
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious director interviews with unexpectedly mundane follow-up.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'm crying about something else. What? This is the last time we'll ever be together.
jokes.setup: Jarmusch being caught crying after eating onion
jokes.punchline: His pretentious explanation about their temporary connection
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation of the Jarmusch character - turning a simple challenge into existential drama.

jokes.text: I get it. Every title means the opposite of what it means. Then I bet I'll love Chernobyl Graveyard.
jokes.setup: Homer figuring out the pattern of indie film titles
jokes.punchline: His confident prediction about enjoying a depressing-sounding movie
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic moment - applying a pattern incorrectly but confidently.

jokes.text: I didn't.
jokes.setup: Homer's confident prediction about loving the depressing movie
jokes.punchline: His simple admission that he was wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy - the delayed punchline to his earlier confidence.

jokes.text: You know these guys? I've never seen them before. Uh... we, uh... He's... He's, uh... he's, uh... That's a joke.
jokes.setup: Family trying to get into Lisa's premiere without proper credentials
jokes.punchline: Their increasingly desperate attempts to explain their connection
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe comedy but somewhat predictable awkward family moment.

jokes.text: This can't be real. It isn't. You crashed and now you're lying unconscious in a snowbank.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing disbelief at being at Sundance
jokes.punchline: Marge's elaborate alternative explanation involving a crash
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark and creative explanation from Marge - good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: Chalmskinn.
jokes.setup: Seeing Chalmers and Skinner together
jokes.punchline: Homer's portmanteau combining their names
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective name combination - shows Homer's pattern recognition in a silly way.

jokes.text: Why... doesn't... life... give me... ladders?!
jokes.setup: Playing Chutes and Ladders and getting another chute
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential complaint about life's unfairness via board game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - turning a simple game frustration into existential crisis. Very quotable.

jokes.text: They're like the family from hell on acid that's on steroids.
jokes.setup: Audience member describing the Simpson family in the documentary
jokes.punchline: The escalating series of modifiers making them sound increasingly terrible
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but the construction is somewhat clunky and predictable.

jokes.text: You did, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what if he walked in barefoot
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction that he did
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag payoff but not particularly memorable or clever.

jokes.text: Brava, Lisa! Shame on Marge! Death to Maggie!
jokes.setup: Audience reacting to the documentary
jokes.punchline: The escalating and increasingly inappropriate reactions, especially to baby Maggie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the absurd 'Death to Maggie' being particularly dark and funny.

jokes.text: I knew you were lame, but I never imagined you were bogus.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing disappointment in Lisa
jokes.punchline: His distinction between 'lame' and 'bogus' as different levels of bad
jokes.explanation: Good Bart voice - his specific slang and the escalation from lame to bogus.

jokes.text: Quirky? Quirky is a grandma who gives people the finger. You made us look like monsters.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending her portrayal as 'quirky'
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific definition of quirky vs their portrayal
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge line with specific, vivid imagery defining 'quirky' - great contrast to their situation.

jokes.text: Landspeeder, do not Bantha, to your nearest cinema
jokes.setup: Reading a positive review of Lisa's film
jokes.punchline: The Star Wars reference replacing normal words in the review
jokes.explanation: Clever Star Wars wordplay but feels somewhat forced and doesn't serve the story well.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. You're too late. I have just hit 'enter,' sending the wi-fi beams along their way. Not if I can help it. Come here, beams, come on. Hey, hey, where you going? Come... Oh, there's too many.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to stop the review from being posted
jokes.punchline: Homer attempting to physically catch wifi signals
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding of technology leading to absurd physical comedy - very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I just said I was thinking of adopting Scarlett Johansson. I mean, is that so wrong?
jokes.setup: Hollywood agent casually mentions controversial statement
jokes.punchline: Treating inappropriate celebrity adoption idea as normal
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Hollywood sleaziness though relies on celebrity name

jokes.text: We want ten percent of the first-dollar gross, full European distribution rights, and access to the most exclusive VIP tent in Sundance.
jokes.setup: Producers asking Skinner to name his price
jokes.punchline: His bizarrely specific and modest demands ending with a tent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from serious business terms to wanting access to a tent - shows Skinner's priorities.

jokes.text: They don't even know that my pockets are full of bottled water and cornbread.
jokes.setup: Skinner celebrating successful negotiation
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been smuggling concession items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment showing his petty victory mindset and strange food choices

jokes.text: They don't even know that my pockets are full of bottled water and cornbread.
jokes.setup: Skinner feeling proud of making a deal
jokes.punchline: His random confession about stealing food and drinks
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and unexpected Skinner moment - the combination of items is particularly funny.

jokes.text: I never wanted to be famous for being mean. I wanted to be famous for catching Santa Claus.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on his unwanted fame
jokes.punchline: His specific alternative goal of catching Santa Claus
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's childish but specific ambitions contrasted with his current situation.

jokes.text: That doesn't sound like your kind of movie. It will be. Check out the poster.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Jarmusch's involvement in Cheaper by the Dozen 3
jokes.punchline: His confidence that he'll make it his style (implied through poster)
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Jarmusch's artistic vision applied to commercial projects.

jokes.text: That's why they call me 'Two Basket' Skinner. What? They do.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining his cautious approach
jokes.punchline: His defensive insistence that people actually call him by this nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner insecurity about his reputation - the defensive 'They do' is perfect.

jokes.text: You're going to be the classiest girl at Classy Girls.
jokes.setup: Nelson's mom preparing him for something
jokes.punchline: The ironic name 'Classy Girls' for what's clearly not classy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Nelson's situation though somewhat predictable in its irony.

jokes.text: If I want that golf umbrella, I got to get to smoking.
jokes.setup: Nelson's mom talking about organizing her cigarette rewards
jokes.punchline: Her motivation to smoke more for a golf umbrella
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cigarette rewards programs - economical and specific to the character.

jokes.text: I like to cry at the ocean, because only there do my tears seem small.
jokes.setup: Nelson's documentary showing his sad home life
jokes.punchline: His poetic but deeply sad observation about crying
jokes.explanation: Beautifully crafted line that's both funny and genuinely touching - perfect character voice for Nelson.

jokes.text: True, but I was also teaching you that festival buzz is, like my movie Coffee and Cigarettes, a funny thing. - Did you see it? - Well, I, uh... That's okay. I know you did.
jokes.setup: Jarmusch making a philosophical point and referencing his own movie
jokes.punchline: His assumption that Lisa saw his movie despite her clear hesitation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing director's ego and self-importance, though not as strong as other Jarmusch bits.

jokes.text: True, but I was also teaching you that festival buzz is, like my movie Coffee and Cigarettes, a funny thing. - Did you see it? - Well, I, uh... - That's okay. I know you did.
jokes.setup: Jarmusch making philosophical point about his film
jokes.punchline: Lisa's awkward admission she hasn't seen it, his confident dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment about pretentious film culture - director's delusion about his audience

jokes.text: And now, like the best independent films, I must fade out with a cool dissolve. That hurts.
jokes.setup: Jarmusch making a pretentious exit speech
jokes.punchline: The reality of his physical departure not matching his artistic vision
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of pretentious director behavior with physical comedy reality check.

jokes.text: We'll be in another movie, and this time we'll act really bad. No more Simpsons movies. One was plenty.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they intentionally act badly in future films
jokes.punchline: Marge's firm declaration against more movies
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-reference to The Simpsons Movie but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: I'm not going to lie to you guys. John C. Reilly would love to be in the Chalmskinn business.
jokes.setup: Skinner reporting on their Hollywood success
jokes.punchline: Name-dropping actor wants to work with their partnership
jokes.explanation: Good callback using the portmanteau name with Hollywood name-dropping

jokes.text: Read it? I lived it. I am Ghost Willie. Check this out.
jokes.setup: John C. Reilly asked if he read Skinner's Ghost Willie script
jokes.punchline: Claims to have lived it and demonstrates
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity cameo joke but relies on recognition of actor's persona

jokes.text: Why can't you be a gentle clinker?
jokes.setup: Chalmers celebrating another success
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific complaint about Skinner's celebration style
jokes.explanation: Good Chalmers character moment with specific vocabulary choice - shows his particular nature.

tmdb_overview: Lisa makes a film about her family that gets accepted by the Sundance Film Festival, and the Simpsons go to Park City, Utah, to attend.
